conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child - Alex, who's 17 years old. They live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in approved education or training. But they have started an apprenticeship recently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have a follow-up question:

Where is Alex doing their apprenticeship - is it in England, or in another part of the UK (Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's in England. They're doing their apprenticeship here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one more question to complete my assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: While Alex is 17 years old and living at home with you, and Child Benefit can normally continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, the rules specifically state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England.

Since Alex has recently started an apprenticeship in England, you would no longer qualify for Child Benefit for them, even though they're still living at home and under 20 years old.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, you should inform HMRC about this change in circumstances, as your entitlement would have ended when the apprenticeship started.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about this decision?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and lives at home with the claimant in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. However, the rules specifically state: 'You'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England.' Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, even though Alex is under 20 and living at home.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old child Alex. While Alex is within the age range where Child Benefit can potentially continue (under 20), Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, regardless of whether they are still living at home and under 20 years old. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
